events.title: Bull Run
events.description: Markets surge on strong earnings and growth expectations.
descriptions: Markets surge on strong earnings and growth expectations.
Risk appetite returns as investors chase growth and momentum.
Indexes grind higher on upbeat guidance and resilient demand.
Equities rally as sentiment flips from cautious to optimistic.
A broad risk-on tone lifts stocks while defensive assets lag.
Corporate results beat forecasts, fueling a sustained bid for shares.
Liquidity stays ample and flows rotate back into equities.
Growth narratives dominate headlines; volatility compresses for risky assets.

events.title: Market Crash
events.description: Global panic. Equities collapse. Bonds and gold benefit.
descriptions: Global panic. Equities collapse. Bonds and gold benefit.
A sharp de-risking wave hits: stocks gap down and correlations spike.
Forced selling and margin calls amplify the drawdown across risk assets.
Credit spreads blow out as investors flee to cash and quality bonds.
Sentiment cracks; headlines speak of capitulation and record outflows.
Safe havens catch a bid while high-beta names lead the decline.
Volatility explodes as liquidity thins and stops trigger a cascade.
Markets reprice recession risk in a matter of sessions.

events.title: Recovery
events.description: Green shoots emerge. Markets bounce back from lows.
descriptions: Green shoots emerge. Markets bounce back from lows.
Stabilization shows up: dips find buyers and breadth slowly improves.
Policy support and calmer data help risk assets repair recent losses.
Investors rotate back from extremes as fear fades from the tape.
A fragile rebound takes hold—still volatile, but the tone is less dire.
Valuations look less stretched after the washout; flows turn cautiously positive.
Corporate credit firms and equities claw back a chunk of the slide.
Hope returns in headlines even as everyone debates if the low is in.

events.title: Inflation Spike
events.description: Inflation surges. Cash and bonds lose real value.
descriptions: Inflation surges. Cash and bonds lose real value.
Hot CPI prints spark debate: sticky prices or a passing shock?
Real yields swing as markets price a tougher inflation path.
Nominal assets wobble; investors reach for inflation-sensitive hedges.
Wage and energy stories dominate—cost-of-living pressure makes the front page.
Central bankers sound more hawkish as expectations drift upward.
Duration-heavy portfolios suffer; shorter credit and real assets catch attention.
The market reprices the path of policy under persistent price pressure.

events.title: Rate Hike Shock
events.description: Central banks hike rates sharply. Bonds and growth stocks suffer.
descriptions: Central banks hike rates sharply. Bonds and growth stocks suffer.
A jumbo move in policy rates catches positioning offside.
The curve reprices fast: long bonds sell off as the hiking path steepens.
Mortgage and credit markets tighten; growth names lead the equity decline.
Officials stress fighting inflation over supporting asset prices—for now.
Liquidity conditions worsen and discount rates jump across risk assets.
Investors dump duration and rethink valuations in a higher-rate world.
Forward guidance turns firm; markets brace for more hikes ahead.

events.title: Stagnation
events.description: Low growth, sideways markets. Boring is the theme.
descriptions: Low growth, sideways markets. Boring is the theme.
Macro data flatlines; ranges tighten and trends are hard to find.
Earnings growth slows but doesn’t collapse—markets drift without conviction.
Investors favor quality and cash flow over speculative stories.
Volatility subsides as nobody expects a breakout in either direction.
A ‘soft patch’ narrative dominates: not a crash, not a boom.
Capital expenditure cools; hiring plans turn cautious across sectors.
Trading desks complain there’s nothing to do—until something breaks.

events.title: Economic Boom
events.description: Strong GDP, full employment. Risk assets surge.
descriptions: Strong GDP, full employment. Risk assets surge.
The economy runs hot: consumption and investment surprise to the upside.
Labor markets look tight and confidence surveys print multi-year highs.
Cyclicals and high-beta sleeves outperform as optimism feeds on itself.
Credit conditions ease and deal-making returns with a vengeance.
Commodity-linked and speculative pockets join the broad advance.
An everything-rally vibe emerges—until someone asks how long it can last.
Growth upgrades land faster than analysts can revise models.

events.title: Tech Sector Boom
events.description: Innovation narratives and AI hype lift growth and tech.
descriptions: Innovation narratives and AI hype lift growth and tech.
Megacap platforms lead; momentum chases anything with a ‘disruption’ story.
Venture and public markets compete for the next category winner.
Semiconductor and cloud names rip as capex intentions surge.
A productivity boom thesis goes mainstream in research notes.
Retail flows pile into thematic ETFs and high-growth names.
Short sellers step aside as trend strength becomes the only trade.
Earnings beats stack up in software and hardware supply chains.

events.title: Recession Fears
events.description: Leading indicators weaken; markets price a harder landing.
descriptions: Leading indicators weaken; markets price a harder landing.
PMI surprises to the downside and the yield curve gets attention again.
Analysts trim GDP forecasts while CEOs warn on order books.
Defensive sectors outperform as cyclicals de-rate.
Credit metrics soften; the market watches delinquencies and spreads.
Consumer confidence slips but hasn’t collapsed—yet.
Headlines oscillate between ‘soft landing’ and ‘recession watch’.
Positioning turns cautious even without a clear catalyst.
